sections.conclusion: Gifts of furnishings for the Governor's mansion, a limousine for transportation, and similar gifts are gifts to the State of California rather than to Governor Deukmejian personally, and need not be reported on the Governor's Statement of Economic Interests if: (1) The State has title to the gifts; (2) The State exercises substantial control over the use of the gifts; (3) The gifts will not become the personal property of Governor Deukmejian after his term of office; and (4) The Governor's Office, Department of Finance, or designated agent notifies the Commission when such gifts are received with a description, donor identification, and estimated value.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether gifts of a limousine, mansion furnishings, and similar items provided by private donors to the Governor must be disclosed on the Governor's Statement of Economic Interests under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that gifts to the Governor for official use (such as a limousine and mansion furnishings) are gifts to the State rather than personal gifts, and therefore need not be reported on the Governor's personal disclosure statement if four conditions are met: State ownership and control, non-personal disposition after the Governor's term, and Commission notification. The FPPC distinguished this case from the Stone opinion based on the permanent value these items retain for the State.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is heavily redacted/damaged with significant text illegibility, particularly in the first page. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers are present; extracted text from narrative content. The Stone opinion reference and four-part test are clearly articulated. Confidence is moderate due to document quality issues but core legal analysis is discernible.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether gifts of a limousine, mansion furnishings, and similar items provided by private donors to the Governor must be disclosed on the Governor's Statement of Economic Interests under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Gifts of furnishings for the Governor's mansion, a limousine for transportation, and similar gifts are gifts to the State of California rather than to Governor Deukmejian personally, and need not be reported on the Governor's Statement of Economic Interests if: (1) The State has title to the gifts; (2) The State exercises substantial control over the use of the gifts; (3) The gifts will not become the personal property of Governor Deukmejian after his term of office; and (4) The Governor's Office, Department of Finance, or designated agent notifies the Commission when such gifts are received with a description, donor identification, and estimated value.

embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter addresses whether gifts of a limousine and mansion furnishings provided by private donors to Governor Deukmejian must be disclosed on his Statement of Economic Interests. The FPPC concluded these are gifts to the State rather than personal gifts and set forth four conditions under which they need not be reported.
